title: Microplastic Pollution
description: Millions of tons of microplastics enter our oceans annually, affecting marine life and entering our food chain.

title: Climate Change
description: Rising global temperatures cause extreme weather events, sea level rise, and ecosystem disruption.

title: Waste Management Crisis
description: Improper waste disposal leads to methane emissions, soil contamination, and resource inefficiency.

title: Overfishing
description: Unsustainable practice of catching aquatic animals—primarily fish—at a rate faster than their populations can naturally replenish.

title: Air Quality Degradation
description: Industrial emissions and vehicle exhaust create harmful air pollution affecting billions worldwide.

title: Biodiversity Loss
description: Habitat destruction and climate change cause species extinction at alarming rates.

title: Ocean Acidification
description: Increased CO2 absorption makes oceans more acidic, harming marine ecosystems.

title: Deforestation
description: Forest destruction reduces biodiversity, increases carbon, and displaces indigenous communities.

title: Agricultural Pollution
description: Chemical fertilizers and pesticides contaminate soil and water, harming ecosystems and human health.
impact: contributing to a 20-30% reduction in crop yields
